meanings.definition: a natural object that covers or envelops
examples: under a covering of dust
the fox was flushed from its cover

meanings.definition: an artifact that covers something else (usually to protect or shelter or conceal it)
the act of concealing the existence of something by obstructing the view of it
examples: the cover concealed their guns from enemy aircraft

meanings.definition: the act of protecting something by covering it
the work of applying something
examples: the doctor prescribed a topical application of iodine
a complete bleach requires several applications
the surface was ready for a coating of paint

meanings.definition: provide with a covering or cause to be covered
examples: cover her face with a handkerchief
cover the child with a blanket
cover the grave with flowers

meanings.definition: form a cover over
examples: The grass covered the grave

meanings.definition: span an interval of distance, space or time
examples: The war extended over five years
The period covered the turn of the century
My land extends over the hills on the horizon
This farm covers some 200 acres
The Archipelago continues for another 500 miles

meanings.definition: provide for
examples: The grant doesn't cover my salary

meanings.definition: act on verbally or in some form of artistic expression
examples: This book deals with incest
The course covered all of Western Civilization
The new book treats the history of China

meanings.definition: include in scope; include as part of something broader; have as one's sphere or territory
examples: This group encompasses a wide range of people from different backgrounds
this should cover everyone in the group

meanings.definition: travel across or pass over
examples: The caravan covered almost 100 miles each day

meanings.definition: be responsible for reporting the details of, as in journalism
examples: Snow reported on China in the 1950's
The cub reporter covered New York City

meanings.definition: hold within range of an aimed firearm
to take an action to protect against future problems
examples: Count the cash in the drawer twice just to cover yourself

meanings.definition: hide from view or knowledge
examples: The President covered the fact that he bugged the offices in the White House

meanings.definition: protect or defend (a position in a game)
examples: he covered left field

meanings.definition: maintain a check on; especially by patrolling
examples: The second officer covered the top floor

meanings.definition: protect by insurance
examples: The insurance won't cover this

meanings.definition: make up for shortcomings or a feeling of inferiority by exaggerating good qualities
examples: he is compensating for being a bad father

meanings.definition: invest with a large or excessive amount of something
examples: She covered herself with glory

meanings.definition: help out by taking someone's place and temporarily assuming his responsibilities
examples: She is covering for our secretary who is ill this week

meanings.definition: be sufficient to meet, defray, or offset the charge or cost of
examples: Is this enough to cover the check?

meanings.definition: spread over a surface to conceal or protect
examples: This paint covers well

meanings.definition: cover as if with a shroud
examples: The origins of this civilization are shrouded in mystery

meanings.definition: copulate with a female, used especially of horses
examples: The horse covers the mare

meanings.definition: put something on top of something else
examples: cover the meat with a lot of gravy

meanings.definition: play a higher card than the one previously played
examples: Smith covered again

meanings.definition: be responsible for guarding an opponent in a game
sit on (eggs)
examples: Birds brood
The female covers the eggs

meanings.definition: clothe, as if for protection from the elements
examples: cover your head!
